steps.title: Tie counts to difficulty
steps.content: The conclusion says seafloor-shell tools were harder to obtain. The table pattern needed to support that is fewer seafloor-shell tools than beach-shell tools at each cave depth, since that would fit beach shells being easier to collect.

steps.title: Reject unrelated table uses
steps.content: Choice B speculates about population size from the depth with the most tools. Choice C reverses the passage by treating beach shells as more durable, even though they are thin and eroded. Choice D speculates about clam populations over time. The remaining choice connects consistent count differences at each depth to collection difficulty: beach shells were easier to collect than shells harvested from the seafloor. The answer is A.
